are numeric and consist of 4 numbers. The first digit indicates the province (except for the 3xxx numbers that are shared by the eastern part of [Flemish Brabant](/wiki/Flemish_Brabant "Flemish Brabant") and [Limburg](/wiki/Limburg_(Belgium) "Limburg (Belgium)"), the 6xxx that are shared between the Hainaut and Luxembourg province, and the 1xxx that are shared by the [Brussels Capital Region](/wiki/Brussels_Capital_Region "Brussels Capital Region"), the western part of [Flemish Brabant](/wiki/Flemish_Brabant "Flemish Brabant") and [Walloon Brabant](/wiki/Walloon_Brabant "Walloon Brabant")). The more zeros there are, the higher the number of inhabitants of that city in the province. For example: [Bruges](/wiki/Bruges "Bruges") (Brugge) is the capital and largest urban centre of the coastal province of [West Flanders](/wiki/West_Flanders "West Flanders") so it gets the 8000 code, the second city is [Kortrijk](/wiki/Kortrijk "Kortrijk") and gets 8500.

["Adressage et affranchissement"](https://www.bpost.be/sites/default/files/pagina/bpost_user_guide_masspost_FR_1_Adressage_et_affranchissement.pdf) [Addressing and postage] (PDF). *[bpost](/wiki/Bpost "Bpost")* (in French). [Bern](/wiki/Bern "Bern"): [Universal Postal Union](/wiki/Universal_Postal_Union "Universal Postal Union"). pp. 24–25.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/96/2_digit_postcode_belgique.png/330px-2_digit_postcode_belgique.png)](/wiki/File:2_digit_postcode_belgique.png)

2-digit postcode areas [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium") (defined through the first two postcode digits)

**Postal codes in Belgium** are numeric and consist of 4 numbers. The first digit indicates the province (except for the 3xxx numbers that are shared by the eastern part of [Flemish Brabant](/wiki/Flemish_Brabant "Flemish Brabant") and [Limburg](/wiki/Limburg_(Belgium) "Limburg (Belgium)"), the 6xxx that are shared between the Hainaut and Luxembourg province, and the 1xxx that are shared by the [Brussels Capital Region](/wiki/Brussels_Capital_Region "Brussels Capital Region"), the western part of [Flemish Brabant](/wiki/Flemish_Brabant "Flemish Brabant") and [Walloon Brabant](/wiki/Walloon_Brabant "Walloon Brabant")). The more zeros there are, the higher the number of inhabitants of that city in the province. For example: [Bruges](/wiki/Bruges "Bruges") (Brugge) is the capital and largest urban centre of the coastal province of [West Flanders](/wiki/West_Flanders "West Flanders") so it gets the 8000 code, the second city is [Kortrijk](/wiki/Kortrijk "Kortrijk") and gets 8500.
When writing the address, the postal code is put in front of the town name.

Special numbers are reserved for the [EU](/wiki/European_Union "European Union") institutions, [NATO](/wiki/NATO "NATO") headquarters, public and commercial [broadcasters](/wiki/Broadcasting "Broadcasting") ([RTBF](/wiki/RTBF "RTBF"), [RTL](/wiki/RTL_Group "RTL Group") [TVI](/wiki/RTL_TVI "RTL TVI"), [VRT](/wiki/Vlaamse_Radio-_en_Televisieomroep "Vlaamse Radio- en Televisieomroep") and [VTM](/wiki/VTM_(TV_channel) "VTM (TV channel)")), the different parliaments and other public institutions.

A correct Belgian [postal code](/wiki/Postal_code "Postal code") is mentioned before the address' municipality or [section](/wiki/Deelgemeente "Deelgemeente") (*e.g.*: "8500 Kortrijk"). While often used erroneously, [Bpost](/wiki/Bpost "Bpost")'s guidelines specifically implore not to mention any [country code](/wiki/Country_code "Country code") in front of the postal code (*e.g.*: "B-8500" instead of "8500"),[[1]](#cite_note-1) similarly to the [Universal Postal Union](/wiki/Universal_Postal_Union "Universal Postal Union")'s S42 definition of an address.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The current 4 digit numeric postal code system has been in place since 1969.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Postal_codes_in_Belgium&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: References")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-1)** ["Adressage et affranchissement"](https://www.bpost.be/sites/default/files/pagina/bpost_user_guide_masspost_FR_1_Adressage_et_affranchissement.pdf) [Addressing and postage] (PDF). *[bpost](/wiki/Bpost "Bpost")* (in French). January 2016. p. 7. Retrieved 2022-08-20. "Ne pas mentionner le code ISO (Belgique) (...) [correct] 6099 Fleurus; [incorrect] B- 6099 Fleurus"
2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** Vivas, Patricia; Lubenow, Joe, eds. (March 2009). "1.2 UPU S42 definition of an address". [*Addressing and Postcode Manual*](https://www.upu.int/UPU/media/upu/publications/manualAddressingAddressingAndPostcodeManualEn.pdf) (PDF). [Bern](/wiki/Bern "Bern"): [Universal Postal Union](/wiki/Universal_Postal_Union "Universal Postal Union"). pp. 24–25.
3. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** De Schutter, Tom. ["Le code postal de la commune : quelles sont les règles ?"](https://www.uvcw.be/population-etat-civil/vos-questions/art-7002). [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20241009073223/https://www.uvcw.be/population-etat-civil/vos-questions/art-7002) from the original on 9 October 2024. Retrieved 24 November 2024.
